definitions: A psychoanalyst who has modified or rejected some of Sigmund Freud's original theories, particularly regarding the emphasis on biological drives and infantile sexuality, while still maintaining a psychodynamic approach.
An individual practicing a form of psychoanalysis that incorporates social and cultural factors more prominently than classical Freudian theory.
examples: Karen Horney was a prominent neo-Freudian psychoanalyst.
The patient sought help from a neo-Freudian psychoanalyst.
Many neo-Freudian psychoanalysts emphasize ego psychology.
